name: #lecture32: How to Map a Revolution: AI & the DNA
description: Why do groups fail to act together, even when their interests align?This lecture explores the hidden dynamics of human coordination, from small teams to entire nations, and why breakdowns are more common than we assume. Drawing on behavioral science, geopolitics, and the study of complex systems, it looks at how trust, emotional bonds, and misaligned incentives shape collective action.The talk unfolds in three parts.Part 1: It begins with the foundations of coordination, examining the psychological and structural conditions that allow individuals and groups to move in sync.Part 2: It then shifts to the geopolitics of emotional bonding, exploring how identity, belonging, and narrative influence decisions at national and global scales.Part 3: Finally, through case studies from regions such as Iran and India, it traces how coordination failures appear in the real world and what they reveal about emerging global patterns.By the end, attendees will have a clearer understanding of why coordination breaks down even among actors who seem aligned, how emotions and identity quietly drive large-scale decisions, and how to better anticipate and respond to these failures across systems, organizations, and societies.Event details:Fort City Brewing, Hauz Khas MarketWednesday, 27 May| 7 PM to 9 PMWhat your ticket includes:1 optional complimentary beer or a mocktailSeating will be on first-come, first-served basis.What to Expect:A 60-minute lecture30–45 minutes of interactive Q&ASocial mixer and personal Q&As with the lecturerAbout the LecturerDr. Raul V. Rodriguez focuses on a critical & increasingly urgent problem: how to empirically measure and understand the ways in which minds, institutions, and societies fail or thrive under conditions of overload, AI pressure, and leadership stress. His research is driven by a unifying question across domains such as elections, markets, organizations, and infrastructure systems: how intelligence and rationality can be formally modeled before the conditions that sustain them begin to degrade.As Vice President of Woxsen University and Sir Cary Cooper Professor of Organizational Behaviour, he combines direct institutional engagement with his work as a computational social scientist modeling systemic instability.About Pint of ViewInspired by the successful Lectures on Tap community in the U.S., Pint of View brings this format to India for the first time. It’s where big ideas meet bar tables, and where you enjoy a drink in hand, walking out with a new way of seeing the world.Book now & develop your own pint of view! 🍻

Expect an insightful panels, a reflective workshop, sharing circles, poetry and collective feminist art. It is something you have never witnessed before!

name: Table of the Levant
description: Table of the Levant is an intimate Eastern Mediterranean supper club in Delhi, built around a long, shared table and a menu inspired by the kitchens of the Levant.We’ll start with a lemon–mint Polo Damascus welcome drink before moving into a generous mezze spread – hummus, mutabbal, labneh, fattoush, Cypriot-style corn, kefta and falafel, with warm pita and plenty of olives and pickles.For the main course, we’ll serve a dramatic Maklouba – a layered, spiced rice and vegetable dish flipped out at the table – followed by a classic ekmek dessert to finish on something sweet and comforting.Seats are limited, the menu is fixed, and the evening is designed for unhurried conversation, shared plates, and a sense of travelling across the Levant without leaving Delhi.

name: Why We Taste With Our Ears
description: This lecture promises to be an evening that rethinks something as simple as eating, by tuning to a sense we rarely notice at the table.About the LectureWhy does the sound of rain make fried food more irresistible? Why does music change how we experience a glass of wine?This session begins with a simple but surprising idea: taste doesn’t start in the mouth, it often begins in the ear.Drawing on extensive research by Bruite Magazine across the subcontinent, this lecture explores how sound quietly shapes the way we anticipate, experience, and remember food. From the rhythm of a bustling street to the curated playlists of a fine-dining restaurant, auditory cues influence everything from perceived texture to indulgence and value.Moving across everyday eating environments and contemporary food spaces, the session explores:The Sound of Anticipation: How auditory cues shape cravings even before the first biteSonic Texture: Why crunch, sizzle, and silence can alter how we perceive flavour and freshnessAtmosphere & Memory: How music, noise, and rhythm influence mood and the way meals stay with usDesigning Food Experiences: The role of sound in restaurants, bars, supper clubs, and immersive dining formatsBlending cultural observation, field research, and sharp storytelling, this session offers a fresh lens on something deeply familiar.Flow of the Evening5:00 PM: Doors open at Siyah Arthouse, Saket: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable5:00 – 5:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations5:30 PM: The learning begins6:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Ms. Ramya6:30 PM onwards: Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversationsAbout the SpeakerRamya G Sri is the Co-founder and Editor-in-Chief of Bruite Magazine, one of South Asia’s leading independent food and culture publications known for its deeply researched, original storytelling and on-ground reporting.Through Bruite, she collaborates with chefs, historians, artisans, and communities across the subcontinent to explore how food intersects with culture, memory, and perception—often through unexpected and underexplored lenses.She also co-leads Bruite Media Co., a specialised brand advisory that has worked with over 500 brands and founders across F&B, lifestyle, D2C, and consumer tech. Her work spans brand launches, communication strategy, and creative training—shaping how brands tell stories and connect with audiences.Her contributions to storytelling have been recognised with the Laadli Media Award for Gender-Sensitive Storytelling and the Abby Silver Award for Advertising. Across her work, she brings together rigorous research, editorial depth, and a strong instinct for how narrative shapes both culture and consumer experience.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Trauma Porn: The Pain We Are Trained To Recognise
description: Join us on Sunday, May 31, for an evening that questions the stories we are drawn to  and what it means when suffering becomes something we consume.About the LectureStorytelling has never been neutral. But what happens when pain becomes the most visible, shareable, and compelling form of narrative?This session explores the growing phenomenon often described as ‘trauma porn’, where suffering is shaped, framed, and circulated in ways that make it legible, gripping, and consumable. In an attention economy driven by intensity and immediacy, trauma can begin to function as narrative currency, promoting dramatic and emotionally charged stories over stories that are quieter, slower, or harder to define.Drawing from trauma theory and cultural analysis, this lecture examines how these patterns extend beyond media and into the way we understand our own lives.Flow of the Evening 4:00 PM: Doors open at Depot 48, GK II: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable4:05 – 4:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations4:30 PM: The learning begins5:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Ms. Gupta5:30 PM onwards: Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversationsAbout the SpeakerVasundhara Gupta is a trauma-focused psychotherapist, narrative practitioner, and the Founder of Humraahi Psychotherapy, a therapist-led mental health practice based in New Delhi. Her work explores the intersection of trauma, storytelling, and the politics of recognition, examining how the narratives available to us shape what we are able to feel, articulate, and survive.Trained at Lady Shri Ram College for Women, University of Delhi, and the Tata Institute of Social Sciences, her therapeutic practice is rooted in relational and process-oriented care, with a particular focus on trauma, grief, identity, and relational complexity.As a narrative practitioner, she is deeply interested in the dominant stories that organise our inner lives, and in what becomes possible when those stories are questioned, re-authored, and collectively re-imagined. Alongside her clinical work, she creates reflective spaces for therapists and peer supporters, and has facilitated workshops, trainings, and support groups across academic and community settings.At the centre of her work is a question she continually returns to: how do we learn to listen to suffering, our own and others’, with greater care, complexity, and responsibility.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen’s, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: M9, Level, block market, 2, M, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Greater Kailash, New Delhi, Delhi 110048, India

name: Whose Art Is It Anyway?
description: Who gets to create, define, and own art?About the LectureArt is often spoken about as something universal, but in reality, art has never existed outside systems of access.This participatory lecture explores how artists from historically marginalised communities navigate spaces where their work is frequently appropriated, diluted, celebrated without context, or dismissed altogether. Drawing from performance and narrative traditions across South Asia, the session asks what shapes artistic legitimacy — and who gets excluded from it.The lecture explores:How caste, class, gender, religion, and sexuality influence who is allowed to create and whose work is valuedWhy certain forms of expression become “mainstream,” while others are labelled “folk,” “obscene,” or “political”How art can function both as a site of erasure and a tool of assertionWhat it means to witness, consume, and engage with art responsiblyThrough examples, discussion, and interactive prompts, participants will reflect on the relationship between art, memory, dignity, and justice.At its core, this lecture invites us to see art not as passive representation, but as a way of negotiating identity, resistance, and belonging.About the SpeakerSri Vamsi Matta is a Bangalore-based interdisciplinary theatre artist whose work is rooted in lived experience, political memory, and anti-caste thought. Born into a Dalit community, his practice engages with everyday and structural violence of caste, positioning art as a space for refusal, repair, and collective imagination.Working across theatre, performance, writing, and participatory formats, his work brings together autobiography, community history, and radical pedagogy. His recent works include Star in the Sky, exploring the emotional and political lives of Dalit students in Indian universities, and Come Eat With Me, a participatory performance examining caste through food and shared meals, staged across India, the United States, and Australia.Vamsi has facilitated workshops, lectures, and teaching engagements across institutions including Azim Premji University, Ashoka University, Northwestern University, and the University of Texas at Austin, bringing anti-caste artistic practice into conversation with diverse audiences nationally and internationally.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: The Blue Moon Sound Bath
description: A rare celestial event. A shared energy. A real shift. The Immersion Room in collaboration with Eka by Paayal invites you into an immersive Blue Moon Sound Bath — a deeply calming evening designed to help you slow down, release emotional heaviness, and reconnect with yourself. Inspired by the symbolic energy of the Blue Moon, this experience becomes a gentle space for reflection, renewal, and inner stillness.As you arrive, you’ll be guided through grounding breathwork, intention setting, and a soothing meditation before sinking into a live sound immersion using healing instruments like sound bowls and chimes. The vibrations create a deeply restorative atmosphere that many describe as calming, expansive, and emotionally releasing.This is not just a meditation session — it is a collective pause from the noise of everyday life. A space where emotions soften, the mind quiets, and the body enters deep relaxation. Whether you are completely new to sound healing or already familiar with wellness practices, the experience is designed to feel accessible, nurturing, and immersive.Come as you are. Leave feeling lighter.

location.address: E5, Market Rd, Block E, DLF Phase 1, Sector 26A, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India

name: The Tiny Hands Art Camp - 10 Days with 10 Mediums
description: Give your child two weeks away from screens and into a space filled with colour, curiosity, imagination, and hands-on making. ✨At Between Easels, children are introduced to 10 different art mediums through playful, guided sessions designed to help them explore freely, think creatively, and build confidence through art. From painting and printmaking to sculpture, collage, and clay — every class becomes a new creative adventure.Led by practicing artists in a warm and encouraging studio environment, the workshop focuses not on perfection, but on expression, observation, experimentation, and joy. 🎨The program concludes with a special exhibition where children proudly display their artworks for friends and family — celebrating everything they’ve created over the two weeks. 🖼️BETWEEN EASELS — KIDS WORKSHOP SCHEDULE10 Days • 10 Mediums • Endless Creativity⏰ Class Timing: 10 AM – 12 PM🎨 Suitable for all skill levels, ages 6+• 1 June (Mon) — Abstract ArtExplore colours, textures, movement, and playful mark-making through expressive painting exercises and creative experimentation.• 2 June (Tue) — Still Life DrawingLearn observation, light, shadows, and basic geometric forms through pencil and charcoal sketching.• 3 June (Wed) — CollagingCreate layered artworks using paper cut-outs, textures, found materials, and imaginative composition techniques.• 4 June (Thu) — Paint Like PicassoExperiment with bold colours, playful portraits, abstract faces, and cubist-inspired visual storytelling.• 5 June (Fri) — MonoprintingDiscover simple printmaking methods to create expressive, one-of-a-kind textured prints.✨ Weekend Off• 8 June (Mon) — Collective Painting & CollageWork together on large collaborative artworks using paint, collage, textures, and mixed media.• 9 June (Tue) — 3D POP SculptureBuild playful three-dimensional forms through sculptural making, construction, and hands-on material exploration.• 10 June (Wed) — Block PrintingDesign and print repeating patterns using carved blocks, shapes, textures, and vibrant inks.• 11 June (Thu) — CollographyExplore texture-based printmaking by layering materials to create embossed and experimental surfaces.• 12 June (Fri) — Clay PortraitsSculpt expressive clay portraits while learning facial structure, textures, and semi-relief techniques.✨ Weekend Off

location.address: A 26/13, A Block, DLF Phase 1, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India

name: Tiffin Trails: Toontooni’s Table x Swarnavo Datta
description: Come enjoy an afternoon of local cuisines across India as Swarnavo and Toonika take you on a culinary journey. Inspired by Swarnavo's book, The Great Indian Tiffin Box, this is a journey you don't want to miss. Share a table with strangers who will soon become friends and break bread together.

location.address: Local Shopping Complex, 11, Vasant Kunj Rd, next to Mini Cooper Showroom, Pocket B-C, Sector C, Vasant Kunj, New Delhi, Delhi 110070, India

name: Aam Nama
description: Aam Nama with Sohail Hashmi is a mango-centric cultural experience celebrating the stories, recipes, and traditions of Old Delhi.The session will be led by renowned historian Sohail Hashmi, who will take guests through the rich history of mangoes, their connection with Delhi’s food culture, and the memories and traditions that surround them. Through engaging storytelling, lesser-known anecdotes, and cultural insights, the session will explore how mangoes have been cherished across generations.The experience will also include heirloom recipes from Old Delhi kitchens, a pocket recipe book launch, and a specially curated mango tasting—bringing together history, flavour, nostalgia and community in one memorable evening at Kathika Cultural Centre, Old Delhi.

location.address: 1237, Gali Khatikan, Imli Mohalla, Mohalla IMLI, Kucha Pati Ram, Sitaram Bazar, Delhi, 110006, India

name: The Therapeutic Art of  Wood Whittling
description: Step into the calming world of carving with The Therapeutic Art of Wood Whittling — a first-of-its-kind hands-on whittling experience in Delhi NCR, hosted at our studio by the incredibly talented artist Viney Anand, who brings over 5 years of wood whittling experience to the craft.Designed for beginners and creative enthusiasts alike, this immersive workshop is all about slowing down, working with your hands, and experiencing the quiet satisfaction of transforming a simple piece of wood into something meaningful. Under Viney’s guidance, you’ll learn the fundamentals of safe wood whittling, carving techniques, tool handling, and finishing methods in a warm and welcoming environment.All tools, safety equipment, and materials are fully included, so you can simply arrive and enjoy the process. By the end of the session, you’ll take home your very own handcrafted wooden project along with a deeply therapeutic and memorable experience.Whether you’re looking to unwind, explore a new skill, reconnect with creativity, or simply experience the joy of slow craftsmanship, this is a workshop you wouldn’t want to miss.

location.address: 2M, 33, Shahid Bhagat Singh Marg, Block M, New Industrial Twp 2, New Industrial Township, Faridabad, Haryana 121001, India

name: ALT EFF Film Club x GuruJal Gurugram | 19th June
description: Join us for a screening of Miles Away; a visual and auditory immersion of the lives of migrant, female brick labors. This film is intimate, cinematic and sensory.Part of our 2024 Program, this was Juror Dia Mirza's reaction:"I was very moved by 'Miles Away' as for the first time I saw the perspective of what Climate Change is doing to women. It was very special to see so many women coming together to tell the story and feeling at ease as they shared personal moments with the female filmmakers. I felt as if I was a part of their lives."To become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date.All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Exclusive screenings of the world's best environmental cinemaAccess to a community of environmentally inclined, film-loving peoplePost-film discussions.Student Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFIf you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 12 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation. With snacks. Always snacks.

location.address: 6/9, F-2, Block F, DLF Phase 1, Sector 26A, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India
